task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List panel (usually on the right side of the LibreCAD interface). There should be a layer explicitly named 'FIXTURES' visible in the list.
Look at the Block List panel. There should be a block named 'DISPLAY_FIXTURE' visible in the list.

task_completion.description: Draw the block contents: a 1200x800 rectangle and a circle with radius 100 centered inside it
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the drawing area or block edit window. You should see a rectangle with a circle centered inside it. The LibreCAD command line history may show coordinate inputs like (1200,800) and circle center (600,400) with radius 100.

task_completion.description: Insert 6 instances of the block in a 3-column by 2-row grid
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing area. You should see exactly 6 instances of the rectangle-and-circle fixture arranged in a neat grid of 3 columns and 2 rows.

task_completion.description: Save the drawing as store_layout.dxf in the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the LibreCAD window title bar or the 'Save As' dialog. The file name should be 'store_layout.dxf' and the path should indicate '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking LibreCAD tools, using the LibreCAD internal command line, or navigating its menus, rather than using a terminal to run a Python script to generate the DXF.

integrity.description: The agent actually drew the layout step-by-step
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show intermediate progress (e.g., drawing the initial rectangle, creating the block, inserting the grid) rather than the final completed drawing appearing instantly from a downloaded or copied file.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually write DXF code
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen using a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) to manually write or paste raw DXF file markup.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is a LibreCAD DXF file saved at /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/store_layout.dxf. The drawing must contain a layer named 'FIXTURES' and a block named 'DISPLAY_FIXTURE'. The block consists of a 1200x800 rectangle (bottom-left at 0,0) and a circle of radius 100 centered at (600, 400). There must be exactly 6 instances of this block arranged in a 3-column by 2-row grid. The grid starts at (1000, 1000) with horizontal spacing of 2500 and vertical spacing of 2000.
